summary: All edge case steps passed. The app properly rejects empty titles (including whitespace-only and null) with clear 400 errors. Emoji and unicode characters are preserved perfectly in both storage and retrieval. Long descriptions (2200+ chars) are accepted and stored in full. HTML/XSS payloads are stored as-is in the database but React's default escaping prevents script execution on the frontend — no dangerouslySetInnerHTML usage was found. Double submit creates two tasks (no idempotency), but the app handles it gracefully without crashes. The API is well-validated for the core cases.

steps.action: Log in as alice@example.com using NextAuth CSRF flow
steps.verification: Got 302 redirect to / with session-token cookie. Session API confirmed user as Alice Johnson with correct email and ID.

steps.action: Create task with empty title (empty string and missing field)
steps.verification: Both empty string title and omitted title field returned HTTP 400 with error 'Title is required'. Validation works correctly.

steps.action: Create task with emoji and unicode title '📋 Tâche spéciale — 任务 #1' and description 'Ñoño → résumé'
steps.verification: Task created with HTTP 201. Retrieved task by ID confirmed exact character preservation: emoji 📋, French accents (â, é), em dash —, Chinese characters 任务, Spanish ñ, and arrow →. No mojibake or encoding issues.
Task created with HTTP 201. Full description preserved in the response without truncation. 50 repetitions of test string all present.

steps.action: Create task with XSS payloads: in title and in description
steps.verification: Task created with HTTP 201. HTML tags stored as literal text in database. Verified no dangerouslySetInnerHTML usage in source code — React's default JSX escaping will render tags as visible text, not executable HTML. XSS safe.

steps.action: Rapid double submit of identical task creation
steps.verification: Both requests succeeded with HTTP 201, creating two separate tasks with different IDs (1ms apart). No crashes, no errors. App handles gracefully though no idempotency protection exists.

break_findings.description: Submitted whitespace-only title ('   ') and null title
break_findings.result: Both correctly rejected with 400 'Title is required' — whitespace trimming validation works
break_findings.fix_suggestion: No fix needed — validation is thorough

break_findings.result: Accepted without any length limit — task created successfully with the full 10K char title
break_findings.fix_suggestion: Add a max length validation on title (e.g., 500 chars) and description (e.g., 10000 chars) to prevent abuse and potential rendering issues

break_findings.description: Submitted SQL injection payload (Robert'); DROP TABLE tasks;--) in title
break_findings.result: Safely handled — Prisma ORM uses parameterized queries so the payload was stored as literal text, no SQL injection occurred
break_findings.fix_suggestion: No fix needed — ORM provides protection

break_findings.description: Submitted invalid date format 'not-a-date' in dueDate field
break_findings.result: Server returned HTTP 500 'Failed to create task' instead of a 400 validation error
break_findings.fix_suggestion: Validate dueDate format before passing to Prisma — return 400 with a clear message like 'Invalid date format' instead of letting the database error bubble up as 500

break_findings.description: Sent two identical POST /api/tasks requests simultaneously
break_findings.result: Both succeeded, creating duplicate tasks. No idempotency protection but no crashes either.
break_findings.fix_suggestion: Consider adding client-side debounce on the submit button, or server-side idempotency keys to prevent accidental duplicates
